game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: ** This survival strategy game plunges players into a glacial apocalypse, where they must lead the last city of survivors, managing resources, assigning jobs, and rebuilding civilization. Core gameplay involves constructing and upgrading buildings, recruiting heroes with unique abilities, developing technology, and engaging in strategic combat to seize resources from beasts and rival chiefs on the ice field. Progression is multi-faceted, encompassing city expansion, technological advancement, hero development, and global competition for the "Strongest" title, often through alliances and player-versus-player battles. As a free-to-play title, monetization allows players to purchase in-game items with real money, such as resource bundles, speed-ups, or hero recruitment packs, to accelerate their progress, though it's not strictly necessary for enjoyment.
game_info.description: Lead the last city of survivors in a frozen apocalypse! Manage resources (food, wood, coal), construct buildings, and recruit heroes to defend against beasts. Balance resource gathering with city expansion while protecting your citizens from the harsh winter and hostile creatures. Survive 10 waves of increasingly difficult beast attacks to prove your city is the strongest and win the game. Your city starts with basic resources - use them wisely to build a thriving civilization before winter claims you all.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate and select buildings/heroes
Space: Confirm selection / Build / Recruit / Upgrade
Z: Cancel current action / Deselect
Shift: Speed up time (hold)
Enter: Start game
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart (return to start screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic resource gathering and building construction. Validates that the player can construct resource buildings and accumulate resources over time without engaging in combat.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Focuses on building food and wood gatherers first, then coal mines. Constructs houses to increase population. Avoids combat and hero recruitment to test pure economic gameplay.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Successfully constructs at least 5 buildings and accumulates significant resources (500+ food, 300+ wood, 200+ coal) within the first 5 waves. Population should increase to at least 20 citizens.

TEST_1.expected: Successfully constructs at least 5 buildings and accumulates significant resources (500+ food, 300+ wood, 200+ coal) within the first 5 waves. Population should increase to at least 20 citizens.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests the optimal winning strategy by balancing economy, military, and defense. Validates that the game can be won by surviving all 10 waves with strategic building placement and hero recruitment.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Builds a balanced economy early (food, wood, coal production). Recruits heroes strategically before waves arrive. Upgrades defensive structures and hero abilities. Uses time acceleration to maximize resource gathering between waves.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Survives all 10 waves of beast attacks, achieves GAME_OVER_WIN state. City maintains positive resource balance throughout. At least 3 heroes recruited and upgraded. Final population above 15 citizens.

TEST_2.expected: Survives all 10 waves of beast attacks, achieves GAME_OVER_WIN state. City maintains positive resource balance throughout. At least 3 heroes recruited and upgraded. Final population above 15 citizens.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests edge case of running out of critical resources (food). Validates that the game properly handles resource depletion and provides meaningful feedback to the player.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Intentionally avoids building food production structures. Spends resources on non-essential buildings. Tests if the game enters a lose state when food runs critically low and citizens starve.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Food resources deplete to zero or near-zero. Game provides visual feedback about starvation. If implemented, triggers GAME_OVER_LOSE when city cannot sustain population or loses too many citizens.
